Lowe's and Home Depot Customers Willing to Spend, Just Not Too Much — Market Talk
data.description: Lowe's and Home Depot each recorded higher ticket sizes in 3Q, a sign that their customers are trading up for more expensive products even as they defer larger remodeling projects due to economic uncertainty and high interest rates. Lowe's and Home Depot depend largely on homeowners, a group that skews more middle and upper income that is still willing to spend, says Piper Sandler analyst Peter Keith tells WSJ. "They still want the newer innovation stuff," says Raymond James analyst Bobby Griffin. "But they're not willing to invest the multiple thousands or six figures in some of these remodel projects," Griffin tells WSJ.(nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

data.title: Fitch Affirms Capital Manor Inc. (OR) at 'BBB-'; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-New York-21 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has affirmed the rating for the following bonds issued by the Hospital Facilities Authority of the city of Salem, Oregon on behalf of Capital Manor Inc. at 'BBB-' :

--$56.4 million revenue and refunding bonds, series 2022;

--$35.4 million revenue bonds, series 2018.

Fitch has also affirmed Capital Manor's Issuer Default Rating (IDR) at 'BBB-'.

The Rating Outlook is Stable.

The 'BBB-' rating primarily reflects Capital Manor's strong revenue defensibility, bolstered by its limited direct competition and favorably strong independent living unit (ILU) occupancy, balanced by a highly leveraged financial profile and modest cash-to-adjusted debt position.

The Stable Outlook reflects Fitch's expectation that Capital Manor will continue to generate sound operating metrics, driven by ILU occupancy stability and consistent service fee increases, which should allow the financial profile to remain resilient through the forward-looking scenario analysis, even in a stress case.

SECURITY

Pledge of, and security interest in, gross revenues of the obligated group (OG), a mortgage lien on the community's property, and a debt service reserve fund.

KEY RATING DRIVERS Revenue Defensibility - 'a'

Strong Demand with Limited Competition

Capital Manor is located in a favorable market (Salem, OR), which has good demographic indicators and limited competition. Capital Manor is the only life plan community (LPC) within 30 miles of its campus and has limited competition for services, which is a primary credit strength. The local community supports strong independent living (IL) demand despite limited draw outside of the primary service area (PSA).

IL occupancy is exceptionally strong with a five-year ILU occupancy average of 98%. Assisted Living (AL) and memory care (MC) occupancy have been stable, averaging 81% each over the past five fiscal years. Demand is sustained by a healthy real estate market with growing median home values. Capital Manor has an average entrance fee of $214,000 and an average net worth of approximately $2.3 million, supporting monthly fee increases in ILUs and ALUs of 4.95% each in 2026. The community also benefits from a sizable waitlist of approximately 400 individuals.

Operating Risk - 'bbb'

Sound and Stable Operating Profile

Capital Manor's 'midrange' operating risk reflects a history of sound and stable operations, which have been offset by higher historical capital investment. Capital Manor's net operating margin (NOM) and NOMA averaged favorable 11.4% and 30.1%, respectively, over the past five fiscal years. In fiscal 2025 (June 30; audited), Capital Manor generated a good 27.8% NOMA, which helped support adequate debt service coverage of approximately 1.9x.

Despite better NOM and NOMA ratios, Capital Manor's operating ratio has been historically weaker, which averaged 100.8% over the past five fiscal years. However, this was improved in fiscal 2025 to 93%, which Fitch views positively.

Fitch's 'midrange' assessment is further bolstered by Capital Manor's higher historical capital investment in plant, which has been robust averaging approximately 140% of depreciation expense over the past five fiscal years. Going forward, management expects capex to be approximately $3 to $3.6 million annually; focusing on routine maintenance needs, which Fitch views as manageable.

Financial Profile - 'bbb'

Very High Leverage Position; Stable Financial Profile with Manageable Capex

Capital Manor's financial profile aligns with a 'bbb' assessment, reflecting stable operations and manageable capital spending despite very high leverage. FY25 unrestricted cash and investments were about $38 million, translating to roughly 40% cash to adjusted debt which is viewed as relatively weaker for an investment grade rating. In the base case, cash to adjusted debt and MADS coverage are supported by anticipated continued improvement in operations into 2030.

Stress assumptions drive an early dip in liquidity, with cash to adjusted debt in the high 30% range, before recovery to around 55% by year five; stress case MADS coverage recovers to roughly 2x and DCOH in the mid-500s in the stress case, consistent with 'midrange' resilience. Capex slightly below depreciation after recent elevated spending supports incremental improvement in liquidity and coverage over the forward look.

Asymmetric Additional Risk Considerations

There are no asymmetric risks associated with the rating.

RATING SENSITIVITIES Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

--Although unanticipated, a material deterioration in cash-to-adjusted debt such that levels are sustained at or below 30%;

--Decline in operational performance leading to deterioration of operating ratio consistently above 100% and or MADS coverage near or below 1x;

--Significant softening of demand to ILU occupancy levels near 85%.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

--Substantial increase in cash flow that improves cash-to-adjusted debt closer to 75%.

PROFILE

Capital Manor is an LPC that has operated in Salem since 1963. The community currently has 295 ILUs (including apartments and duplex/triplex villa and townhome ILUs) and a licensed residential care facility (RCF) configured with 83 AL beds and 34 MC beds. Capital Manor does not have a SNF but is able to provide high levels of care in its RCF, which is open only to its residents.

Capital Manor, Inc., which owns the community, is the sole member of the OG and had total operating revenues of approximately $30.9 million in fiscal 2025. The community has been managed by Life Care Services LLC (LCS), a national senior living operator, under a management agreement since 2010; LCS employs the community's executive director. The agreement was last renewed for a term of five years in 2020 which management expects to extend for another 5 and a half years by this January.

Sources of Information

In addition to the sources of information identified in Fitch's applicable criteria specified below, this action was informed by information from DIVER by Solve.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. ESG Considerations

The highest level of ESG credit relevance is a score of '3', unless otherwise disclosed in this section. A score of '3' means ESG issues are credit-neutral or have only a minimal credit impact on the entity, either due to their nature or the way in which they are being managed by the entity. Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores are not inputs in the rating process; they are an observation on the relevance and materiality of ESG factors in the rating decision. For more information on Fitch's ESG Relevance Scores, visit https://www.fitchratings.com/topics/esg/products#esg-relevance-scores.

data.title: Gold steady as traders ramp up December rate cut bets
data.description:
Key points:
  • Fed's William says central bank could cut rates in near term
  • Traders see 74% chance of US interest rate cut in December
  • Wall Street's main indexes gain

By Pablo Sinha

Gold prices held steady on Friday, after falling over 1% earlier in the session, as traders boosted bets on a December U.S. interest rate cut following dovish U.S. Federal Reserve comments.

Spot gold was steady at $4,086.57 per ounce, as of 01:48 p.m. ET (18:48 GMT), after falling more than 1% earlier in the session. Bullion is set for a weekly gain of 0.1% so far.

U.S. gold futures for December delivery settled 0.5% higher at $4,079.5 per ounce.

New York Fed President John Williams on Friday said the U.S. central bank could still trim interest rates in the near term, without jeopardizing its inflation goal.

The comments "are certainly supportive ... it did give the gold market bulls some friendly fodder early today," said Jim Wyckoff, senior analyst at Kitco Metals.

Traders now see a 74% chance of a rate cut at the Fed's next meeting, compared to 40% earlier in the day. (FEDWATCH)

The delayed jobs report showed a mixed labor market picture, with nonfarm payrolls rising by 119,000 in October, well above forecasts for a 50,000 gain, while the unemployment rate climbed to a four-year high.

Gold, a non-yielding asset, tends to do well in low-interest-rate environments.

Meanwhile, other Fed members maintained a hawkish stance, with Dallas Federal Reserve President Lorie Logan calling for leaving the policy rate on hold "for a time."

Traders are also keeping a close eye on U.S. stock markets as "if the stock market rallies stronger today, that's probably going to put downside pressure on gold because of the keener risk appetite in the marketplace," Wyckoff added.

Wall Street's main indexes gained as traders boosted bets on an interest rate cut by the Fed next month following remarks from policymakers.

Meanwhile, physical gold demand across major Asian markets remained weak this week, as volatility in rates deterred potential buyers from making purchases.

Elsewhere, spot silver fell 0.4% to $50.39 per ounce, platinum rose 0.1% to $1,512.67, and palladium edged 0.2% up to $1,380.

data.title: Elanco's drug gets emergency nod to treat deadly flesh-eating parasite in cats
data.description:
The U.S. Food and Drug Administration on Friday authorized emergency use of Elanco Animal Health's chewable tablet to treat the infestation of a parasite in cats known as New World screwworm.

Shares of the company were up more than 3% in afternoon trading.

The agency said it is the first time it has cleared a drug specifically for this use in cats and kittens. The authorization allows the drug, branded as Credelio CAT, to be used while U.S. health officials consider New World screwworm a potential public health emergency.

The New World screwworm is a parasitic fly species whose larvae infest open wounds on warm-blooded animals, including livestock and humans.

Most cats in the U.S. are at low risk of New World screwworm due to their geographic location, the agency said. Outdoor cats near the U.S.-Mexico border and cats that have traveled to countries where the parasite is found are more likely to be exposed.

Credelio CAT will only be available with a prescription from a licensed veterinarian. The FDA said professional expertise is needed to watch for side effects and manage any problems that arise.

Cat owners should speak with their veterinarian about whether the treatment is suitable for their animal, the FDA said.

Credelio CAT was first approved by the FDA in 2019 to prevent fleas and ticks in cats and kittens.

The drug's active ingredient, lotilaner, is part of a widely used class of drugs for parasites. While these products are considered safe for most cats, they have been linked in some cases to nervous system side effects such as tremors, unsteady movement and seizures, the regulator said.

data.title: PLATTS: 100--US House passes bills to ease LNG export approvals, study refining challenges
data.description: (Platts)--21Nov2025/215 pm EST/1915 GMT **Bill aims to prevent future LNG export restrictions **Study would address declining petrochemical refining The US House of Representatives on Nov. 20 advanced a bill to boost US LNG shipments by eliminating a statutory requirement for the Department of Energy to find that export licenses are in the national interest. The measure, which would give the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission exclusive authority to permit LNG terminals, cleared the House on a 206-188 vote with support from a regionally diverse group of 11 Democrats. Fifteen Democrats and 13 Republicans did not vote. Sponsored by Representative Austin Pfluger, Republican-Texas, the bill — called the Unlocking our Domestic LNG Potential Act of 2025 — was passed by House Republicans in the previous Congress but stalled in the Democrat-controlled Senate. House Republicans also sought to include provisions to ease LNG export restrictions in the GOP's sweeping budget reconciliation bill in the wake of the Biden administration's controversial LNG pause. Those measures were ultimately stripped out in response to adverse rulings from the Senate parliamentarian. The Biden administration's pause applied to new DOE authorizations for LNG exports to countries that lack free trade agreements with the US. The non-FTA approvals are key for project developers because such countries represent most of the world's LNG imports. The moratorium forced several US LNG developers to delay their targets for launching new projects and created uncertainty about the long-term market share of US supply. The policy was quickly rescinded under the Trump administration, supporting a boom in final investment decisions on new US liquefaction capacity this year. Six US LNG projects have reached FID so far in 2025, representing a total liquefaction capacity of around 60 million metric tons/year. Still, House Energy and Commerce Committee Chairman Brett Guthrie, Republican-Kentucky, argued during Nov. 20 floor debate that Pfluger's bill would prevent future presidents from arbitrarily blocking LNG deliveries to US allies. The bill "would ensure this catastrophic policy decision is never made again by removing DOE from the statutory authorization process," Guthrie said. The legislation would maintain presidential authority to impose sanctions on foreign governments by prohibiting LNG exports if justified, he added. Representative Frank Pallone, Democrat-New Jersey, the committee's ranking member, countered that terminating DOE's public interest determinations would "allow unlimited amounts" of LNG to be sent abroad while driving up consumer energy bills. The US Energy Information Administration's latest Short-Term Energy Outlook forecasts Henry Hub natural gas spot prices to average $4/MMBtu in 2026, or 16% higher than 2025, Pallone noted. The agency said the increase is being driven primarily by "increased LNG exports amid flat production growth." The latest North American Natural Gas Short-Term Outlook by S&P Global Energy CERA analysts estimated average Henry Hub prices higher at an average $4.59/MMBtu for 2026. STUDY ON US REFINING CHALLENGES The House also voted 230-176 to advance the REFINER Act, a bill sponsored by Representative Bob Latta, Republican-Ohio, that would require the National Petroleum Council to conduct a study on US petrochemical refining capacity. The measure was backed by 25 House Democrats. Latta noted that the number of US refineries continues to dwindle California, which was home to more than 40 refineries in the 1980s, had 14 operating refineries as of last year and two of those facilities are scheduled for closure in early 2026, he said. The REFINER Act would require the National Petroleum Council's report to include information on how US refineries contribute to US energy security. The report must also assess "any state or federal executive actions, regulations or policies that have caused or contributed to a decline in US petrochemical refining capacity," Latta said. Pallone called the report unnecessary. He noted that a 2020 report by the Government Accountability Office found that US refinery margins began to suffer after Congress voted in 2015 to repeal the federal ban on crude oil exports. "You don't need a refinery if you can export the crude, but this report doesn't address that at all," Pallone said. — Zack Hale, zack.hale@spglobal.com; Corey Paul, corey.paul@spglobal.com-- Edited by Meghan Gordon, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Fitch Rates Battery Park City Authority, NY's $662MM Ser 2025 Senior Rev Bonds 'AAA'; Outlook Stable
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-New York-21 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has assigned a 'AAA' rating to the following Battery Park City Authority, NY (BPCA) revenue bonds:

--$662,285,000 million senior revenue bonds, series 2025 (sustainability bonds).

The bonds are expected to be priced via negotiated sale during the week of Dec. 1. Proceeds will be used to fund certain capital projects related to the authority's sustainability program and resiliency projects, and to refund all or a portion of the authority's outstanding 2023 junior notes and 2025 junior notes.

In addition, Fitch has affirmed the following BPCA ratings:

--Outstanding $666 million senior lien revenue bonds series 2023A, B&C and $223 million series 2019A, B&C at 'AAA';

--Outstanding $118.5 million junior lien revenue bonds series 2019D-1 and series 2019D-2 (and associated bank bonds) at 'AA+'.

The Rating Outlook is Stable.

The 'AAA' and 'AA+' ratings on the senior and junior lien bonds, respectively, reflect solid growth prospects for pledged revenues, derived primarily from changes in assessed property values, and expected strong resilience to revenue declines, which offset concerns about concentration risk of commercial office properties and the financial services sector. The potential for higher leverage of the junior lien, a lower permitted coverage cushion and the risks associated with office and industry concentration contributes to the slightly weaker resilience for the junior lien bonds and warrants the one-notch distinction.

Fitch's dedicated tax analysis considers full leveraging up to the authority's general resolution additional bonds test (ABT) for both the senior and junior liens, despite the potentially lower issuance resulting from New York City's role in oversight and the approval of BPCA's debt issuance. The tests essentially allow bonds if recent pledged revenues cover pro forma maximum annual debt service (MADS) by 2x for senior lien bonds and 1.55x for junior and senior lien bonds. Inclusive of the series 2025 senior bonds, pro forma coverage from fiscal 2024 pledged revenues cover aggregate senior MADS (occurring in 2028) approximately 2.7x, and aggregate senior and junior lien MADS 2.6x.

Pledged revenues are estimated to improve in fiscal 2026, with coverage increasing to 2.9x of pro forma senior lien MADS and to 2.8x for aggregate senior and junior lien MADS. Additional leveraging plans to support the authority's $2 billion sustainability program will likely keep future coverage levels in the near to medium term close to those based on fiscal 2024 pledged revenues depending on the future structuring of debt service and use of the junior lien. The program and the authority's investments are designed to further protect Battery Park City and its assets and other sections of downtown Manhattan against sea-level rise, storm surge, and other climate events. Fitch believes these actions will improve future sustainability and resilience of the area to climate risks supporting the attractiveness of the area and limiting downside pressure on assessed values due to storm-related events.

RATING SENSITIVITIES Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

For both liens:

--A significant and sustained decline in billable assessed value (AV), driven by lower office or hotel occupancy, demand for residential properties or other factors, that reduces pledged revenues weakening resilience of the security structure and/or Fitch's view of future revenue growth prospects;

--Additional debt issuance resulting in maximum annual debt service coverage sustained at levels closer to the ABT of the respective liens could alter our view of the revenue base and pressure the resilience assessment and ratings on the bonds.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

For the senior lien bonds:

--An upgrade is not possible because the bonds are already rated at Fitch's highest level.

For the junior lien bonds:

--Continued solid pledged revenue growth accompanied by evidence that the additional junior lien debt issuance will not result in coverage that is materially lower than the amount allowed under the senior lien bonds' ABT.

Dedicated Tax Security

The senior lien bonds are special obligations of the authority. They are payable from pledged revenues that include primarily payments in lieu of real property taxes (PILOTs), calculated based on property assessed values, and non-PILOT base rents from the leasing and subleasing of parcels in Battery Park City (BPC), net of the authority's operating expenses. The junior lien bonds lien on pledged revenues is subordinate to the senior lien on pledged revenues.

Dedicated Tax Key Rating Drivers

Growth Prospects for Revenues - 'aa'

Sound Revenue Growth Prospects: Fitch believes pledged revenues will continue growing but at a somewhat slower rate than historically due a moderation of changes in assessed values and will be temporarily boosted by the phase-out of property tax abatements and the potential impact of renegotiated ground leases. Fitch expects growth to be at a pace between inflation and national economic growth over the longer term, consistent with an 'aa' assessment for growth prospects and considers the desirability of the location, the relative affordability of office space compared to other areas in the city, and the long tenor of underlying leases.

Sensitivity and Resilience - 'aaa'

Robust Resilience: Strong debt service coverage and limited historical pledged revenue volatility provide the structure with a large cushion against economic stress, including risk to declining real estate valuations, mitigating concerns about pledged revenue concentration. Resilience is consistent with a 'aaa' assessment for both the senior and junior lien bonds.

Exposure To Related Government

Fitch believes BPCA's operating risk is minimal, as operating expenditures are modest relative to its revenue base. Therefore, Fitch has not assigned an Issuer Default Rating (IDR) to the authority. BPCA was created by, and is a component unit of, the state of New York (IDR AA+/Stable).

New York City has oversight and approval authority over capital planning and debt issuance. However, neither the state nor city government has appropriation control over the authority's operating budget or debt service payments. The city's claim to residual pledged revenues is only after operating expenses and annual debt service funding requirements are satisfied.

STRUCTURE RESILIENT IN ECONOMIC CYCLES

Fitch evaluates the sensitivity of the dedicated tax revenues to cyclical revenue declines by comparing the cushion provided by the bond structure, namely the additional bonds test (ABT), to the largest decline in revenues over the period covered by the revenue sensitivity analysis (fiscal 1999 to present). The largest cumulative revenue decline was 5.9% between fiscal years 2000 and 2002. Fitch believes this decline was mild considering the time period includes the events of Sept. 11, 2001.

Over the last decade (through fiscal 2024), net pledged revenues increased 4.0% on a compound average annual basis. Residential pledged revenues grew 5.9% annually, with office revenues at 2.8%. Fiscal 2025 pledged revenues (exclusive of operating costs) are projected to show only moderate annual growth. Pledged revenues are estimated to increase by 3% in fiscal 2026. Overall billable assessed values (AVs) for fiscal 2024 were up 3.9% following 7.5% growth in 2023, due to an increase in office values. Billable AVs for fiscal 2025 and 2026 are up only 1% yoy reflecting moderate growth of 1.2% in office values and moderate increases in residential values. Billable AVs reflect NYC Department of Finance practice of five-year smoothing of annual changes in assessed values.

If the authority were to fully leverage both liens, fiscal 2024 pledged revenues could decline 50% before becoming insufficient to pay senior lien debt service and 35% for junior lien debt service. These declines are equal to 8.5x and 5.9x the largest cumulative historical net pledged revenue decline of 5.9%. This performance provides a solid cushion for a larger decline to occur with results still consistent with a 'aaa' assessment of financial resilience for both liens.

Pledged revenues from fiscal 2024 residential properties alone cover senior lien pro forma MADS of $112.6 million 1.7x and total aggregate MADS 1.6x partially mitigating the financial services sector and commercial office component of the pledged revenues. There has been recent material diversification in both BPC tenants and downtown resident employment, but Fitch expects the area will continue relying on the financial services industry.

Management plans to issue additional debt over the next four years to support continued efforts for climate resiliency projects in the BPC area. Fitch expects future issuances will be structured to exceed the minimum ABT requirements and that pledged revenues will continue to experience at least inflationary growth supporting improved future coverage levels. However, should the actual bond structure, timing of issuance and ultimate aggregate MADs coverage deviate from Fitch's expectations or revenue performance deviates from Fitch's expectations, this could result in negative rating pressure.

PROFILE

BPCA is a New York state public benefit corporation that owns and manages the project area containing BPC, a 92-acre complex of office, hotel, and residential condominium and rental apartment buildings situated on the Hudson River waterfront in downtown Manhattan. The authority leases and subleases individual parcels in BPC pursuant to long-term net ground leases and subleases. All such ground leases and subleases expire on June 17, 2069, except for the Gateway Plaza Sublease (the largest residential property) which expires on June 30, 2040, but can be extended by the lessee until 2069. Ownership of the BPC site was conveyed to the authority by New York City in 1982.

Battery Park City contains 10.7 million sf of office space, retail space, a marina, two hotels, a multiplex cinema and 8,300 rental and condominium residential units. There are five public schools and approximately 36 acres of parks and public spaces. It is home to approximately 17,000 residents, 45,000 workers, and a half-million annual visitors, according to the authority. Residential properties accounted for 53% of pledged revenues in 2024 with office properties contributing 44% and hotels 3%.

data.title: /C O R R E C T I O N — Cisco Systems, Inc./
data.description:
In the news release, Cisco Invests in Spatial Intelligence Pioneer World Labs, issued 20-Nov-2025 by Cisco Systems, Inc. over PR Newswire, we are advised by the company that changes have been made. The complete, corrected release follows, with additional details at the end:

Cisco Invests in Spatial Intelligence Pioneer World Labs

World Labs is redefining what's possible by giving AI the ability to generate, reason within, and interact with 3D worlds

News Summary

  • Cisco Investments is investing in World Labs, which aims to accelerate the development of Large World Models (LWMs) that enable AI systems that can perceive, understand, and interact with the physical world in 3D.
  • World Labs is at the frontier of spatial intelligence, developing foundational AI models that bridge the gap between virtual and real-world environments.
  • This investment underscores Cisco's role as the critical infrastructure provider for the AI era.

SAN JOSE, Calif., Nov. 20, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — Cisco today announced that Cisco Investments, its venture capital arm, is investing in World Labs Technologies, Inc., a spatial intelligence AI startup. Founded by Dr. Fei-Fei Li, a renowned computer vision pioneer often called the "godmother of AI," World Labs is driving the next paradigm shift in artificial intelligence. The company enables machines to understand and interact with 3D environments using human-like spatial intelligence, with the potential to transform industries such as gaming and robotics.

"The next great platform evolution in AI will be built around spatial intelligence," said Jeetu Patel, President and Chief Product Officer, Cisco. "By joining Fei-Fei and the world-class World Labs team on this journey, we are accelerating the development of a future where AI takes an active role in the physical environment with agents and devices capable of transforming industries, augmenting human capacity, and driving massive gains in productivity."

"World Labs is driven by a single passion: advancing AI to augment human potential and deliver real-world impact," said Fei-Fei Li, Co-founder and CEO, World Labs. "Cisco's leadership in secure, scalable infrastructure is a critical enabler for the next generation of AI and the evolution of physical AI. We are thrilled to work with them as we bring our vision to life."

"The evolution of AI from linguistic intelligence to spatial intelligence marks the next revolutionary leap, as models move beyond understanding language to reasoning about, generating, and interacting with the physical world. World Labs is pioneering this frontier with Large World Models," said Martin Casado, General Partner at a16z. "We've long shared Fei-Fei's vision for spatial intelligence and see huge potential for changing how AI engages with the world. Cisco's investment reflects the importance of secure, scalable infrastructure in advancing this new era of AI."

This strategic investment in World Labs underscores Cisco's commitment to its multi-pronged innovation strategy encompassing R&D, investments, M&A, and strategic partnerships across the AI landscape. By supporting World Labs' pioneering work in developing LWMs, Cisco continues to reinforce its role as the critical infrastructure provider for the AI era, empowering customers globally to harness the power of secure, reliable, and trustworthy AI across digital and physical applications.

About Cisco

Cisco is the worldwide technology leader that is revolutionizing the way organizations connect and protect in the AI era. For more than 40 years, Cisco has securely connected the world. With its industry leading AI-powered solutions and services, Cisco enables its customers, partners and communities to unlock innovation, enhance productivity and strengthen digital resilience. With purpose at its core, Cisco remains committed to creating a more connected and inclusive future for all. Discover more on the Cisco Newsroom.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release may be deemed to contain forward-looking statements, which are subject to the safe harbor provisions of the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. These statements include, among others, statements regarding Cisco's future financial or business performance, strategies or expectations. Readers are cautioned that these forward-looking statements are only predictions and may differ materially from actual future events or results due to a variety of factors, including, among other things, the ability of Cisco to achieve expected benefits of its investments, business and economic conditions and growth trends, increased competition, global economic conditions and uncertainties in the geopolitical environment and other risk factors set forth in Cisco's most recent reports on Form 10-K and 10-Q, respectively. Any forward-looking statements in this release are based on limited information currently available to Cisco, which is subject to change, and Cisco will not necessarily update the information.

data.title: Stocks Are Having a Tough Month. But the AI Bubble Is Down — Not Out. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Teresa Rivas

It's been a tough month for the market, especially tech stocks. Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang even mentioned the B-word in his post-earnings remarks this week, if only to decisively shoot down the idea of a bubble.

At present, worries about the huge amounts being spent on artificial intelligence are high enough that another great Nvidia quarter wasn't enough to buoy stocks or dispel bubble talk . Stocks were broadly lower the day after it reported, and Friday's bounce isn't enough to erase recent losses.

Some might say that is a good thing: The market had gone up so quickly and smoothly after the tariff-related spring swoon that bears are worried stocks have gotten ahead of themselves and are far too pricey.

Gavekal Research's Charles Gave argues that in one sense the current situation looks worse than the dot-com bubble. At that time a winner-take-all environment meant investors were acting rationally when buying a bunch of companies with relatively fixed costs, knowing one would eventually see sales explode and become dominant in their industry — bringing their stock along for the ride.

By contrast, today companies aren't spending money up front and then sprinting to grow sales, but rather making a small number of sales in between investing huge amounts of capital with no apparent end point. Not to mention the U.S. doesn't have enough electricity to power them.

"All this suggests that for AI companies, unlike dot-coms, the marginal cost of sales is relatively high, and the potential profit margin correspondingly thin," Gave writes. So if 1999-2000 was a rational bubble, "2025 looks like an irrational bubble, and a capital-intensive irrational bubble to boot. In other words, it is a bad bubble."

So to an extent, the market could be self-correcting on the same premise now, given all the focus that's come on the tightknit AI economy. Even with stocks climbing Friday, the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite are still down for the week.

At the same time, there are reasons to think that this is a temporary setback for the market general, and tech specifically, even as the recent tech skepticism means the market is still on track for its worst month since March.

Wells Fargo analyst Ohsung Kwon put out his 2026 outlook, and predicts that the S&P 500 will end the year at 7800, nearly 20% higher than today. While stocks are expensive, earnings are growing as well, making those valuations look less outlandish.

Yet more to the point, he thinks AI stocks will be the big winners in the second half of the year, because no one in power can afford a bear market.

"Equities are now a bigger part of US household net worth than real estate and investment income tax could be as big as a quarter of government revenue," he writes. "A K-shaped economy led by wealth effect means a bear market could trigger an economic downturn, which neither the Federal Reserve nor the Government can afford especially into midterms."

The upshot is the Fed is likely to boost liquidity — the firm is forecasting it will purchase $25 billion in Treasuries a month beginning in April.

"Historically, ample liquidity has helped Tech more than other sectors," he notes. "We expect increased speculation and risk-on as more liquidity gets injected to the system, resulting in a potential AI bubble trade."

There's the B-word again. But since there's plenty of time before it's likely to become a problem, it can wait until another day.

Write to Teresa Rivas at teresa.rivas@barrons.com

ESALQ Corn Analysis
data.description: corn analysis CORN/CEPEA: Prices are stable in Brazil, but drop in the US Cepea, 21 - Sellers are away from closing trades, since they are focused on summer crop activities. Thus, corn prices are firm in many regions surveyed by Cepea. As for the demand, agents close deals when they need to replenish inventories. Abroad, values are moving down, influenced by estimates indicating high global production between 2024/25 and 2025/26 crops. However, price drops were limited by the sharp international demand for the US product. The ESALQ/BM&FBovespa Index for corn prices rose only 0.3% in the same comparison, to close at BRL 67.64 per 60-kg bag on Nov. 19. In the accumulated of November, the Index has increased 2.3%. On the average of the regions surveyed by Cepea, between November 13 and 19, corn values upped 0.3% in the wholesale market (deals between processors) and 0.1% in the over-the-counter market (paid to farmers). ESTIMATES - The USDA released again its supply and demand report (it was suspended since September this year, due to the shutdown). The Department says that the world production may drop a little compared to the September report, due to the smaller supply in the United States. In spite of that, the US supply may hit the record of 425.52 million tons, against 427.10 million tons in September. As a whole, the world production is forecast at 1.28 billion tons in the 2025/26 season, 4.5% more than in the previous. The global consumption is projected at 1.29 billion tons, above the 1.28 billion tons estimated in September. In 10 producing days of November, Brazil shipped 2.67 million corn tons (Secex). In case this pace continues, total exports are likely to hit 5 million tons. Data from Cepea indicate that the average in Paranaguá (PR) increased 0.4% from Nov. 13-19 and 0.3% at the port of Santos (SP). The US dollar upped 0.8% in the same period, closing at BRL 5.338 on Nov. 19. CROPS - The summer crop planting had reached 52.6% of the total in Brazil until November 15, a progress of 4.9 percentage points in one week (Conab). For the Portuguese version please refer to or click on <ESQ/CORN/ANALYS>

data.title: Highest Earners Show No Sign of Slowing Down Spending — Market Talk
data.description: Big spending from the highest earners is propping up the economy and Truist economist Mike Skordeles says that can continue for a long time. Wage growth has accelerated across income categories, but upper levels of the economy have a leg up with equity markets continuing to surge and home values having soared over the last five years. That has helped the top 10% of earners spend uninhibited by inflation, Skordeles tells WSJ. "The upper end spending being more robust and having those wealth effects is masking…flat to sluggish spending for lower income consumers," Skordeles says. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

data.title: Tom Lee’s BitMine to begin offering annual dividend as ETH treasury mNAV dips
data.description:
The largest Ethereum treasury company, BitMine Immersion Technologies, said it will become "the first large-cap crypto company to declare an annual dividend," as the firm posts its first earnings report since the crypto market pullback in the second half of the year that has seen digital asset treasuries struggle.

BitMine will offer an annual dividend of $0.01 per BMNR share. The stock is trading around $26.49, up slightly on the day, but down significantly from a yearly high watermark of $135 set in early July, shortly after the firm announced its ETH acquisition strategy, according to The Block’s price page.

The dividend, payable on Dec. 29, is BitMine's latest attempt to return shareholder value through traditional equities engineering. In July, the firm became one of the first DATs to approve a share buyback plan to supplement its ongoing ETH purchases.

Backed by leading investors including ARK's Cathie Wood, DCG, Founders Fund, Galaxy Digital, Pantera, and individual investors like Wall Street titans Bill Miller III and Tom Lee, BitMine is the second-largest crypto treasury firm after Strategy, and by far the largest public ETH-focused DAT.

The firm recorded $328 million in net income for its fiscal year ending Aug. 31, equating to $13.39 in fully diluted earnings per share, according to Friday's release. It holds nearly $10 billion worth of ETH — 3.55 million tokens purchased at an average price of around $3,120.

Negative mNAV

ETH is trading around $2,730 on Friday, according to The Block's data, indicating BitMine’s multiple to Net Asset Value (mNAV) has fallen below 1.0x. With ETH sitting near multi-month lows, the company sits on an unrealized loss of roughly $4.52 billion.

A representative for a competing ETH treasury firm, the Ether Machine, told The Block that the outlook for crypto DATs that acquired tokens via at-the-money issuances is "grim."

"The way in which BitMine (BMNR) and Sharplink (SBET) have raised over $10B to buy more ETH over the last 6 months is a capital lever that breaks under the market conditions we find ourselves in now, leaving retail shareholders effectively exposed to a greater loss than if they just purchased ETH," the representative said.

According to Ether Machine’s calculations, "an August BMNR purchaser is down ~73%, compared to an outright ETH purchaser during that time frame, who would be down ~30%," considering that ETH was above $4,000 for much of that month.

Of course, BitMine is far from the only DAT to decline in value amid a weakening crypto market. The Block reportedly earlier Friday that the combined market capitalization of crypto treasury firms has plunged from $176 billion in July to roughly $99 billion today.

Despite BMNR’s performance, BitMine Chairman Tom Lee said the firm is "well positioned in 2026." BMNR is down nearly 50% over the past 30 days, though still up about 258% year-to-date.

The firm’s staking solution — dubbed the Made in America Validator Network, or MAVAN — will debut in the first quarter, boosting the firm’s mining operations. BitMine noted it operates Bitcoin mining operations in Trinidad and Texas.

data.title: PLATTS: 100--New investment schemes renew interest in Mexico's power sector, but not without risks
data.description: (Platts)--21Nov2025/203 pm EST/1903 GMT **Investment schemes draw interest but critics flag risks, legal flaws **Experts advise using current rules despite potential changes Mexico's power sector is showing some dynamism after years of paralysis as the government of President Claudia Sheinbaum introduces a set of investment schemes to develop power generation and transmission infrastructure. Observers told Platts there is plenty of appetite, but according to critics, the new regulation has flaws and is heavily influenced by political will, which can quickly shift and could even be challenged, adding extra risk to projects. 6 GW OF NEW CAPACITY IN 25 LOCATIONS The government has invited private participants to develop roughly 6 GW of new capacity in about 25 "strategic" locations. The aim is to ensure supply while increasing the amount of clean energy generated in the country. The government promised a simplified process for obtaining permits rapidly. Lawyers representing energy clients told Platts some of those projects have already been approved, but declined to identify their clients. The final list of approved projects is expected to be announced in December or early in 2026. The government is also looking to attract interest from the industry through a scheme called Productor de Largo Plazo (long-term producer), which observers say resembles the former figure of Productor Independiente de Energia (PIE for its Spanish acronym or independent energy producer). Under this scheme, companies build power plants under a long-term supply contract with the state utility CFE, which can be the only buyer. At the end of the contract term, CFE can opt to keep the plant. The scheme is likely to be successful in areas where there is currently high demand that is not being served, such as the country's two peninsulas. Companies also have the option to develop their own energy. The government made some adjustments to the way companies can do this, introducing a new legal figure called "autoconsumo", which replaces the former scheme called "autoabasto". Under this scheme, companies producing up to 0.7 MW will not require a permit, and those bigger than 0.7 MW and up to 20 MW will receive preferential treatment when obtaining permits, the government said. Observers noted that this scheme is attracting significant attention as it effectively addresses the immediate needs of companies and alleviates bottlenecks. Those above 20 MW will receive no preferential treatment and should follow the normal legal procedure, which may take longer. "There is a lot of appetite for self-supply," said Cesar Diaz Sacal, an expert in the Mexican energy sector at law firm Galicia, adding that although it will not solve all the energy requirements of the industry, it's a good "bandage" for the system. Diaz Sacal mentioned that these projects will likely be conventional natural gas-fired combined cycles and cogeneration, but the scheme could also be appropriate for solar projects with storage infrastructure. "People are looking at Mexico once again," said Daniel Sanchez, a lawyer specialized in the Mexican energy sector at law firm Baker McKenzie, noting that the interest is coming from different types of companies, including those that had previously operated in the country and then left. NOT WITHOUT RISKS Although the new rules may be drawing attention from the industry, critics complain the process is flawed and warn that risks are high. "Given the large amount of requirements that had to be presented within a tight timeline, some clients believe there is no way that companies could deliver all correctly and completely unless they had prior understanding with the government," said Miriam Grunstein, an expert in the Mexican energy sector at consultancy Brilliant Energy, noting that if only a handful of participants close to the government are invited to participate, the country risks having concentrated markets. Other observers stated that the recently published secondary laws, particularly those related to self-supply, exceed the provisions of the law, rendering them potentially illegal and thus jeopardizing the projects authorized under those laws. "The 'reglamento' violates article 30 of the energy sector law, which only allows the holder of a generation permit to use the energy produced by a plant and not others," said Luis Izquierdo, an independent consultant specialized in the Mexican power sector. Izquierdo pointed out that although the government may proceed with issuing permits under these conditions, there is a risk that those projects may be cancelled or at least challenged under a future administration with a different view. USE WHAT YOU HAVE "The risk exists, but it is not a major risk," said Rosanety Barrios, an independent consultant who served as official during various administrations, referring to the possibility that a new administration could cancel projects. "The reglamento was signed directly by the Mexican president, so it is hard to challenge," Barrios said, noting that self-supply in Mexico has always operated under a gray area. Barrios said that there are other issues in Mexico that worry her more, like the reduced autonomy of the judicial system, which is largely controlled by the executive branch. "There is a risk that the regulation could be changed. And if they change it, the literal text of the law does restrict the use of energy to the permit holder, but I do not see this as a risk that would prevent companies from pursuing self-supply projects," said Valeria Vazquez Maulen, a partner at law firm Venture Lex, who specializes in the Mexican energy sector. In practice, I would not tell a client: 'Don't move forward with your self-supply project because the regulation or law might change.' Honestly, I don't see it that way today," Vazquez said Vazquez explained that in a rule-of-law environment, authorities should respect acquired rights, meaning projects that are already operating. Any regulation that seeks to change those conditions would need to include a transitory regime, she said. Just as legacy contracts were preserved in previous administrations, if tomorrow the regulation changes, a new one would need to incorporate transitional norms to preserve the rights of those who already have a permit or established conditions, she said. "My message to clients is this: play with the rules we do have, and protect yourselves as best you can. Don't jump into the void without financial safeguards, hedges, or a solid legal and regulatory strategy. But ultimately, these are the rules on the table," she said. — Sheky Espejo, sheky.espejo@spglobal.com-- Edited by Giselle Rodriguez, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Fitch to Rate Sculptor CLO XXXVll, Ltd.; Publishes Presale Report
data.description:
Fitch Ratings-Chicago-21 November 2025:

Fitch Ratings has assigned expected ratings and Rating Outlooks to Sculptor CLO XXXVll, Ltd.

Sculptor CLO XXXVII, Ltd.

----A-1 ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----A-2 ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; AAA(EXP)sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----B ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----C ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; A+(EXP)sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----D-1A ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; BBB(EXP)sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----D-1B ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; BBB(EXP)sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----D-2 ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; BBB-(EXP)sf; Rating Outlook Stable

----E ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

----Subordinated Notes ; Long Term Rating; Expected Rating; NR(EXP)sf

Transaction Summary

Sculptor CLO XXXVll, Ltd. (the issuer) is an arbitrage cash flow collateralized loan obligation (CLO) that will be managed by Sculptor CLO Management LLC. Net proceeds from the issuance of the secured and subordinated notes will provide financing on a portfolio of approximately $400 million of primarily first lien senior secured leveraged loans.

KEY RATING DRIVERS

Asset Credit Quality: The average credit quality of the indicative portfolio is 'B+/B', which is in line with that of recent CLOs. Issuers rated in the 'B' rating category denote a highly speculative credit quality; however, the notes benefit from appropriate credit enhancement and standard CLO structural features.

Asset Security: The indicative portfolio consists of 97.81% first lien senior secured loans and has a weighted average recovery assumption of 73.85%. Fitch stressed the indicative portfolio by assuming a higher portfolio concentration of assets with lower recovery prospects and further reduced recovery assumptions for higher rating stresses.

Portfolio Composition: The largest three industries may comprise up to 38% of the portfolio balance in aggregate while the top five obligors can represent up to 12.5% of the portfolio balance in aggregate. The level of diversity required by industry, obligor and geographic concentrations is in line with that of other recent CLOs.

Portfolio Management: The transaction has a 5.1-year reinvestment period and reinvestment criteria similar to other CLOs. Fitch's analysis was based on a stressed portfolio created by adjusting the indicative portfolio to reflect permissible concentration limits and collateral quality test levels.

Cash Flow Analysis: Fitch used a customized proprietary cash flow model to replicate the principal and interest waterfalls and assess the effectiveness of various structural features of the transaction. In Fitch's stress scenarios, the rated notes can withstand default and recovery assumptions consistent with their assigned ratings.

The WAL used for the transaction stress portfolio is 12 months less than the WAL covenant to account for structural and reinvestment conditions after the reinvestment period. In Fitch's opinion, these conditions would reduce the effective risk horizon of the portfolio during stress periods.

RATING SENSITIVITIES Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Negative Rating Action/Downgrade

Variability in key model assumptions, such as decreases in recovery rates and increases in default rates, could result in a downgrade. Fitch evaluated the notes' sensitivity to potential changes in such a metric. The results under these sensitivity scenarios are as severe as between 'BBB+sf' and 'AA+sf' for class A-2, between 'B+sf' and 'BBB+sf' for class C, between less than 'B-sf' and 'BB+sf' for class D-1, and between less than 'B-sf' and 'BB+sf' for class D-2.

Factors that Could, Individually or Collectively, Lead to Positive Rating Action/Upgrade

Upgrade scenarios are not applicable to the class A-2 notes as these notes are in the highest rating category of 'AAAsf'.

Variability in key model assumptions, such as increases in recovery rates and decreases in default rates, could result in an upgrade. Fitch evaluated the notes' sensitivity to potential changes in such metrics; the minimum rating results under these sensitivity scenarios are 'AA+sf' for class C, 'A+sf' for class D-1, and 'Asf' for class D-2.

USE OF THIRD PARTY DUE DILIGENCE PURSUANT TO SEC RULE 17G -10

Form ABS Due Diligence-15E was not provided to, or reviewed by, Fitch in relation to this rating action.

REFERENCES FOR SUBSTANTIALLY MATERIAL SOURCE CITED AS KEY DRIVER OF RATING The principal sources of information used in the analysis are described in the Applicable Criteria. REPRESENTATIONS, WARRANTIES AND ENFORCEMENT MECHANISMS A description of the transaction's representations, warranties and enforcement mechanisms (RW&Es) that are disclosed in the offering document and which relate to the underlying asset pool is available by clicking the link to the Appendix. Offering documents for this market sector do not typically include RW&Es that are available to investors and that relate to the asset pool underlying the security. However, the offering document for this transaction included a draft of the indenture as an appendix, which contains RW&Es related to the underlying asset pool. For further information, please see Fitch's Special Report titled 'Representations, Warranties and Enforcement Mechanisms in Global Structured Finance Transactions'.

data.title: FDA Approves Merck's Keytruda with Padcev to Treat Bladder Cancer — Update
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

The Food and Drug Administration approved Merck's Keytruda in combination with Padcev to treat certain adults with muscle invasive bladder cancer (MIBC).

The FDA said Friday it has approved the drug combination as a treatment for before and after bladder-removal surgery in adult MIBC patients who are ineligible for cisplatin, a chemotherapy medication.

Padcev is co-developed and commercialized by Pfizer and Astellas Pharma. Pfizer said the FDA's approval of the drug combination was granted months earlier than expected.

A recent trial demonstrated statistically significant improvements in event-free survival and overall survival for patients with previously untreated MIBC who were treated with the drug combination before and after bladder-removal surgery and pelvic lymph node dissection, compared with surgery alone, the FDA said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Dow Rallies; Bitcoin Extends Declines — WSJ
data.description:
By Caitlin McCabe

Stocks turned higher Friday, with the Dow industrials clawing back the previous session's losses, after Federal Reserve policymaker John Williams made the case for a near-term cut to interest rates.

The gains cap a volatile week in which investors have debated lofty technology valuations and aggressive artificial-intelligence spending plans. The Dow led benchmarks higher, rising more than about 1.5%, more than 700 points.

But the rally hasn't been enough to make up the sharp declines earlier in the week. All three major indexes are still down more than 1% over the five-day period. Recent investor anxieties have dinged high-flying tech stocks and other assets seen as risky, such as bitcoin, which remained under pressure Friday. The Cboe Volatility Index, Wall Street's fear gauge, ebbed, but remained at an elevated level near 24.

Earlier global chip stocks, including TSMC and ASML, had sold off, echoing Thursday's declines in the U.S. and weighing on country indexes. Benchmarks in China, Japan and South Korea lost more than 2%.

In other markets:

U.S. Treasury yields dropped to 4.07%.

The yen strengthened after Japan's cabinet approved a $135 billion economic stimulus package.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).
